question.content: Two identical smooth conducting rings overlap at points A and C in a uniform magnetic field that disappears rapidly. Use overlap geometry and branch-current symmetry to obtain each ring speed magnitude. Answer format: one final result only. If the result naturally simplifies to a single value, give that value; otherwise give a single algebraic expression using the symbols defined in the problem. Use ASCII characters only. Do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign. Answer format: a single velocity expression or numerical value; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Use alpha=pi/3 to fix overlap geometry and lens area S=R^2*(alpha-sin(alpha)).
step_1.key_point: The overlap lens controls branch electromotive asymmetry.

step_3.content: Solve branch currents between A and C, then use J=∫B*I*Rdt and ∫B*(-dB/dt)dt=B^2/2.
step_3.key_point: Two rings obtain equal speed magnitudes in opposite directions.
